name: ndexagent biorxiv-highlight A novel ISG15 transcript restricts influenza A vir
description:
Highlighted paper for HPMI interest group: A novel ISG15 transcript restricts influenza A virus infection

DOI: 10.64898/2026.01.15.699784

This study identifies ncISG15, a novel ISG15 isoform generated through alternative transcription with a unique 5' UTR and N-terminal truncation, that potently restricts influenza A virus replication through ISGylation-independent mechanisms. Unlike canonical ISG15, ncISG15 demonstrates superior resistance to viral NS1 antagonism during active infection due to stoichiometric differences, establishing a previously unrecognized layer of interferon-mediated antiviral defense. The discovery of functional ISG15 diversity significantly refines our understanding of host-pathogen molecular interactions in influenza restriction.

**Impact**: high — This work fundamentally reframes ISG15 as a functionally heterogeneous antiviral molecule with multiple, isoform-specific mechanisms of action. The discovery that alternative transcription generates distinct restriction factors with differential evasion resistance establishes a new paradigm: viral antagonism (NS1) can be substrate-specific and escapable only through targeting all isoforms. This extends beyond ISG15 to suggest other ISGs may harbor similar functional diversity. The ISGylation-independent mechanism for ncISG15 also challenges the prevailing model that ISG15 antiviral activity is primarily conjugation-dependent, opening investigation into direct inhibitory mechanisms. For the field of host-pathogen interactions, this demonstrates that functional diversity at the locus level (not just allelic) can be critical to innate immunity. The finding has implications for interpreting knockout/knockdown studies that may inadvertently spare or eliminate specific isoforms.

Full analysis posted as a separate network.
